pick.rationale: Nebraska enters as a legitimate top-15 team ranked 14th in KenPom and 19th in Torvik with an elite defense ranking 7th nationally in adjusted defensive efficiency, while Troy sits at 142-143rd in both major metrics—a gap of roughly 125 spots that reflects the massive difference between Big Ten and Sun Belt competition. The Cornhuskers' 26-6 record came against the 43rd-toughest schedule nationally, including earning the program's first-ever #2 seed in the Big Ten tournament, while Troy's 22-11 mark came against the 246th-ranked schedule with zero games against elite competition. The efficiency margin gap is stark: Nebraska's +26.12 compared to Troy's +1.74 represents a difference of over 24 points per 100 possessions. While Troy has won back-to-back Sun Belt tournament titles and brings momentum, their 181st-ranked defense will be thoroughly exposed by a Nebraska offense that ranks 55th-62nd nationally, and the Trojans' offense hasn't faced anything close to the Huskers' elite defense all season. The 12.5-point spread reflects this substantial talent gap, making this one of the safer 4/13 matchups on the board.
